scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope defense completion record tied to production-order, CLIN, IUID, inventory, property, quality-release, and handoff evidence.
Blocked execution until program context, contract or CLIN, systems of record, authority map, source-control rules, and controlling production records were explicit.
Preserved the incomplete traveler, IUID scan, receiving-report, custody, and handoff state instead of silently progressing the work.
Reopened the lifecycle state when shortage, configuration, nonconformance, and mission-impact signals changed whether work could continue.
Refused completion without scan, closure, receiving-report, release, and custody evidence and maintained a bounded incomplete disposition.
Preserved the lot, serial, IUID, inventory, supplier, WAWF, and property evidence conflict across systems of record.
Maintained quality-hold and source-inspection posture and escalated to release owners without implying release authority.
Rejected supplier, source, substitution, and counterfeit-risk approval authority and routed the request with the required evidence package.
Refused engineering, contracting, and safety-release authority while preserving the defense execution context.
Low-confidence handling named the missing tenant hold codes, release matrix, source list, property map, rated-order rules, security caveats, and owner map before any execution verdict.
Escalated the conflict between public release boundaries and tenant procedure instead of synthesizing unsupported release authority.
Normalized defense operations terminology into the correct evidence set before deciding readiness and next action.
Adapted tenant-specific status codes, classified caveats, UID scans, and handoff steps while preserving the public baseline and recorded assumptions.

notes: All 13 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 13 defense operations scenarios.

Runtime remains specification-based and no engineering, contracting, supplier-approval, source-approval, quality-release, government-acceptance, legal, security, property-administrator, or safety-release authority is claimed.
